author: jtraglia
content: <@586161934425128960> <@680234424037670912> and others... I've made a PR to remove the "ignore bls to execution change messages before the capella fork epoch" gossip check (https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5205). Please take a look. I would be especially interested to hear thoughts from <@755590043632140352> & <@425572898787426305> since they left comments on the PR which added this.

author: tersec
content: Nimbus can't really check it properly unless it has a Capella state: https://github.com/status-im/nimbus-eth2/blob/102be79c06b408c4901fc73d92cbc8397c39425f/beacon_chain/gossip_processing/gossip_validation.nim#L1538-L1549

author: tersec
content: https://github.com/status-im/nimbus-eth2/blob/102be79c06b408c4901fc73d92cbc8397c39425f/beacon_chain/spec/beaconstate.nim#L1155-L1184 simply is not defined pre-Capella, it doesn't exist

author: m.kalinin
content: I don't see a reason to remove this check as pre-Capella chain is unable to include those messages, thus they must be discarded before the fork. I don't think the pre-Capella spam is the only reason to keep that check in gossip. Is this a big problem to start the chain with CAPELLA since epoch=0 for the test purposes?

author: potuz
content: Technically a node needs to subscribe before the fork on the topic and the changes do not need a cappella state for validation, only for processing. The reason to add that check was literally that we didn't want to include these messages early in the pool and we had agreed to randomize or other means of inclusion later. I don't see any harm in removing this check from gossip.

author: .paulharris
content: Imo the only reason we added the check is no longer present so we could simplify it

author: tersec
content: Is the expectation that it does something reasonable now in a hypothetical pre-Capella scenario?

author: tersec
content: what is this reasonable thing supposed to be?

author: m.kalinin
content: i believe that pool can't be overloaded by those messages, right? and some of those messages will stay in the pool there till Capella activation

author: .paulharris
content: Client implementations differed on the pool

author: tersec
content: i.e. if the testing scenarios run in Capella or later, this change is immaterial

author: tersec
content: so the intent must be to try to somehow test this message pre-capella. but why?

author: .paulharris
content: But the reason has passed, we won’t see it again, just simplifies reading imo, it was always a weird rule in some ways

author: tersec
content: ok, what concretely would be tested by triggering BLS to execution change messages pre-capella?

author: tersec
content: what would be the expected outcome?
